purpose: Design the additional validation required before the PR #132 production acceptance threshold proposal for player_history_production_feature_v0 can be accepted, amended, or rejected. Validation-design/report only; does not accept the threshold and does not authorize production binding.
decision_rationale: Direct inspection of the committed TIBER-Data promoted artifact and its build script establishes that no season earlier than 2022 exists anywhere in TIBER-Data's governed data for this contract family. Under the N=3 input-window design used by every prior experiment in this chain, the only target season with a full 3-prior-season trailing window in currently-promoted data is 2025 itself -- the season already evaluated. Both compared validation options (second-target-season replay, rolling-origin backtest) are blocked on the same missing prerequisite: at least one additional governed prior season (2021) does not yet exist. This is an evidenced gap with a specific next step, not an unresolved design question and not a rejection of the validation design itself.

validation_options.description: Re-run the #112/#122 three-arm design (baseline/real/shuffled) with a different target season, holding the design otherwise identical.
validation_options.required_source_seasons: target season S2 plus 3 prior seasons (S2-1, S2-2, S2-3), N=3 per PR #128 section 2.3
validation_options.input_window_semantics: identical to existing design: season < S2, no partial-season substitution, QB/RB/WR/TE, REG only
validation_options.source_artifact_requirements: TIBER-Data promoted player_season_coverage_v0 (or successor) must cover S2 as outcome and S2-1..S2-3 as input, same governed/promoted discipline as the existing 2022-2025 artifact
validation_options.mirror_generation_requirements: new outcome + input mirror pair analogous to PR #120's, for the new season pair
validation_options.replay_report_requirements: new controlled-rerun report and contract-instance validation report analogous to #122/PR #130, explicitly dated/labeled for S2, never overwriting the 2025 reports
validation_options.missing_history_reporting: independently computed for the S2 population; must not assume the 2025 no-history count/share (125/610) carries over
validation_options.reuse_of_pr130_infrastructure: contract schema/type is season-generic already (player_identity_join_keys.season is a plain number); generator/replay scripts (playerHistoryContractV0Replay.ts, runPlayerHistoryContractV0Replay.ts) hardcode CONTRACT_V0_TARGET_SEASON=2025 and CONTRACT_V0_INPUT_SEASONS=[2022,2023,2024] as module constants and would need parameterization; the controlled-rerun script/module (scripts/runPlayerHistoryPromotedControlledRerun.ts, src/rehearsal/playerHistoryPromotedControlledRerun.ts) this option's replay_report_requirements depend on carries the same hardcoding (fixed mirror paths, fixed REPORT_DATE, and two season-specific TARGET_OUTCOME_KEYS entries) and must be parameterized alongside them -- generator-script work, not a contract schema amendment
failure_modes: S2-1..S2-3 not available under governed/promoted status -- blocks entirely (the actual current state)
materially smaller joined population for S2 than 2025's 485/610, weakening statistical power
silently reusing the 2025 shuffle seed/lambda without re-justifying for a new population, changing the design rather than replicating it
validation_options.estimated_governance_complexity: comparable to the #117-#122 chain repeated for one new season: TIBER-Data promotion extension, Forecast mirror-refresh issue, Forecast controlled-rerun issue. Known shape, not small, but proven once already.

validation_options.description: Evaluate multiple target seasons (e.g. 2023, 2024, 2025), each predicted from its own 3 prior seasons, as independent origins.
validation_options.required_source_seasons: the full governed span must cover each origin's own outcome/target season AND its 3 prior input seasons -- e.g. targets {2023,2024,2025} need outcome seasons {2023,2024,2025} plus input seasons {2020,2021,2022,2023,2024}, a combined 2020-2025 span (6 seasons for 3 origins). Of that span, 2022-2025 already exist in the current promoted artifact; 2020 and 2021 are the new/missing seasons.
validation_options.input_window_semantics: identical per-origin semantics to option A, repeated per origin; each origin's model fit/imputation must not see another origin's held-out rows
validation_options.source_artifact_requirements: same governed/promoted discipline as option A, over a wider season span
validation_options.mirror_generation_requirements: one outcome+input mirror pair per origin, independently gated and reported
validation_options.replay_report_requirements: one controlled-rerun report per origin plus a rollup presenting per-origin results side by side, explicitly NOT averaged into a single number
validation_options.missing_history_reporting: independently per origin; rollup may show trend across origins informationally, not as an aggregate pass/fail gate
validation_options.reuse_of_pr130_infrastructure: same as option A but scripts need genuine multi-invocation parameterization, somewhat more script work
failure_modes: same as option A's failure modes, compounded across origins
treating a rolling-origin result as stronger evidence than warranted when origins are close in time and may share correlated season-level shocks rather than being truly independent draws
validation_options.estimated_governance_complexity: substantially larger than option A: widest source-season span of any option here (2020-2025, 6 seasons for 3 origins vs 2021-2024's 4 seasons for option A), multiplies the mirror-refresh/controlled-rerun pattern by the number of origins

recommendation_rationale: Option A is the smaller, more tractable next step: exactly one additional governed season beyond what exists, reuses the already-validated experimental design, produces one clean additional data point. Option B is the stronger eventual validation but has a strictly larger and more uncertain source-data prerequisite and should be considered only after option A succeeds or is shown infeasible for a different reason. Both options are blocked today on the same missing prerequisite (see source_availability_feasibility).
validation_invariants: target season S uses only seasons < S -- no exceptions, for whichever S is being validated
no target-season or partial-target-season substitution, under any feature-availability fallback
the real-vs-baseline-vs-shuffled framing is required for every origin's evaluation
production_only remains the v0 default feature-family scope for every origin unless a separate, explicitly-reviewed amendment clears the PR #132 section 3.6 full-set added-value bar
source identity lock and committed-mirror source-identity verification (PR #130) must pass independently for whatever artifact snapshot backs each origin
missing-history players must not be silently zero-filled -- entire feature block stays null, for every origin
no-history subgroup count/share/by-position must be reported per evaluated population, independently per origin
deterministic replay/report generation, reproducible from committed inputs, for every origin
no Fantasy/product consumer behavior, for any origin's evaluation
no production model behavior -- remains rehearsal/non-production infrastructure regardless of origin count

components_from_pr_132.full_set_added_value_bar: > 2% relative joined-MAE improvement over production_only, plus governance test, if full-set scope reconsidered

threshold_component_evaluation.aggregation_rule_rationale: Averaging across origins would let one strong origin mathematically compensate for one weak or failing origin -- the same 'masking' failure mode PR #124 and PR #132 both warn against in different forms. A signal that only clears the bar on average, but fails outright on one evaluated season, has not demonstrated the season-to-season robustness additional validation exists to test for. A future implementation issue proposing averaging instead would need to make an explicit case this document does not make and does not recommend.
threshold_component_evaluation.note: With only one additional origin (option A), pass/fail is a single per-origin outcome; the aggregation rule becomes load-bearing starting at 2+ additional origins (option B).

tiber_data_current_coverage.evidence: direct inspection of the committed promoted artifact at merge 65fb498

tiber_data_build_script_scope.evidence: SEASONS = [2022, 2023, 2024, 2025] constant, explicit docstring scoping to 2022-2025 per TIBER-Data #190 approved scope / PR #189 source boundary

earlier_season_search.result: exactly one substantive '2021' hit: a player's rookie_year metadata field, not a season-2021 data row
earlier_season_search.conclusion: no governed or candidate 2021 (or earlier) season data exists anywhere in TIBER-Data today

source_availability_feasibility.methodology_compatibility_note: The NFL adopted its current 17-game/18-week regular-season format starting in the 2021 season, the same format the existing 2022-2025 build already assumes in its coverage_status methodology (weeks_observed thresholds reasoned explicitly about 'the full week span of a 2021+ season'). A 2021 promotion would likely be methodology-compatible with the existing artifact without a separate season-length carve-out. A pre-2021 season (16-game/17-week format, needed for option B's wider span) would need its own methodology consideration this document does not attempt to resolve.
source_availability_feasibility.raw_source_availability: not determined by this document -- whether nflreadpy.load_player_stats()/load_players() actually return complete, governable 2021 rows is a TIBER-Data-side source-verification question, not something this Forecast-side design can or should determine on TIBER-Data's behalf

forecast_side_generator_readiness.contract_schema_type: already season-generic (player_identity_join_keys.season is a plain number); no contract amendment needed for a new target season
forecast_side_generator_readiness.generator_and_mirror_scripts: hardcode target season (2025) and input window ([2022,2023,2024]) as module constants, and bake 2025 into output file names and boundary-statement keys (e.g. contains_no_2025_outcome_values) in playerHistoryRunPopulationMirrors.ts, playerHistoryPromotedMirrorRefresh.ts, and playerHistoryContractV0Replay.ts -- generalizing requires generator-script parameterization, not a schema change
forecast_side_generator_readiness.controlled_rerun_script_and_module: scripts/runPlayerHistoryPromotedControlledRerun.ts hardcodes OUTCOME_MIRROR_REL to the ..._2025.promoted_outcome_mirror.json path, INPUT_MIRROR_REL to the ..._2022_2024.promoted_input_mirror.json path, and a fixed REPORT_DATE='2026-07-04' baked into its output report paths; src/rehearsal/playerHistoryPromotedControlledRerun.ts additionally hardcodes TARGET_OUTCOME_KEYS=['ppr_2025_actual','season_ppr_2025','target_outcome','target_season_ppr'], two of which are season-specific. Option A's required controlled-rerun report (see validation_options[0].replay_report_requirements) depends on this path, so it must be included in the same parameterization work as the mirror/replay generators -- a follow-up that parameterized only those generators would still be unable to produce the required second-season metrics/report.
forecast_side_generator_readiness.evidence: direct inspection of the named Forecast source files in this repository

next_allowed_issue.title: Assess source availability for player-history additional validation
next_allowed_issue.scope_note: Determine, on the TIBER-Data side using TIBER-Data's own approved source-verification process, whether nflreadpy.load_player_stats()/load_players() return complete, governable 2021 REG-season rows for QB/RB/WR/TE, and if so scope a TIBER-Data candidate-build + promotion review analogous to #190-#193 for a 2021 extension. Must not perform a Forecast mirror refresh, controlled rerun, or any option A Forecast-side step -- those remain gated behind a successful source-availability outcome. Must not expand scope to option B's wider (2020+) requirement unless a future threshold-review issue calls for it.
forbidden_in_next_issue: seasonalPprModel.ts wiring, in whole or in part
production feature use of any kind
treating the PR #132 threshold as accepted

non_negotiables_preserved: target season S may only use seasons < S -- no exceptions, for whichever season is validated
no target-season or partial-target-season substitution, under any feature-availability fallback
missing-history players must not be silently zero-filled -- entire feature block stays null
source artifact identity must be path + sha256 + promotion review, never sha256 alone; mismatch at generation time fails closed
the real-vs-baseline-vs-shuffled validation framing remains required for every origin evaluated
this document does not authorize seasonalPprModel.ts wiring, in whole or in part
this document does not accept the PR #132 threshold proposal
any Fantasy/product consumer requires its own separate, explicitly-approved review
this document does not promote or demote any TIBER-Data artifact
non_goals: does not implement any additional validation
does not accept, amend, or reject the PR #132 threshold proposal
does not run a production-path leakage audit
does not modify seasonalPprModel.ts or any other production Forecast file
does not promote or demote any TIBER-Data artifact, and does not perform TIBER-Data's own source-verification process on its behalf

does not claim 2021 (or any other season) data is confirmed available -- only that no governed data earlier than 2022 currently exists, and that a 2021 extension appears methodology-compatible based on the existing build script's own documented assumptions

failure_modes_prevented.failure_mode: guessing 2021 data exists and building mirrors against an artifact that was never actually promoted
failure_modes_prevented.prevention: source_availability_feasibility states directly, from inspection, that no season earlier than 2022 exists in TIBER-Data today

failure_modes_prevented.failure_mode: jumping straight to a rolling-origin backtest without checking whether even one additional season is available
failure_modes_prevented.prevention: recommendation prioritizes option A first and defers option B's larger (2020+) requirement

failure_modes_prevented.failure_mode: averaging multi-origin results and letting one strong season mask a failing one
failure_modes_prevented.prevention: threshold_component_evaluation.aggregation_rule sets per-origin pass/fail as the default

failure_modes_prevented.failure_mode: treating 'the schema didn't need to change' as 'the scripts didn't need to change'
failure_modes_prevented.prevention: forecast_side_generator_readiness separates the season-generic contract schema from the 2025-hardcoded generator scripts

failure_modes_prevented.failure_mode: treating this document's source-feasibility finding as authorization to proceed with mirror-building anyway
failure_modes_prevented.prevention: next_allowed_issue scopes the next step to source-availability assessment only

failure_modes_prevented.failure_mode: letting additional-validation design become a backdoor to leakage-audit or model-wiring scope
failure_modes_prevented.prevention: non_negotiables_preserved and next_allowed_issue.forbidden_in_next_issue restate the existing boundary explicitly

next_step: The decision permits opening a SEPARATE 'Assess source availability for player-history additional validation' issue (see next_allowed_issue), scoped to TIBER-Data's own source-verification process for a 2021 extension. It does not permit a Forecast-side mirror-refresh/controlled-rerun issue yet, a production-path leakage audit design/execution issue, or a seasonalPprModel.ts wiring issue.
